channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Virginia Court Strikes Down Redistricted Voting Map in a Huge Blow to Democrats
item.description: The decision is a major victory for Republicans, wiping away a measure approved by voters to allow Democrats to gain as many as four House seats in the midterms.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson, Nick Corasaniti and Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Steve Earley/The Virginian-Pilot, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The ruling from the Virginia Supreme Court leaves Democrats at a disadvantage in the country’s redistricting fight.

item.title: 4 Takeaways From the Virginia Supreme Court Gerrymandering Decision
item.description: The ruling was a major lift to the G.O.P. efforts to build a structural advantage through redistricting.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Win Mcnamee/Getty Images
item.media:description: Voters at a polling location in Arlington, Va., on April 21, 2026.

item.title: Trump Gave Out a No-Bid Contract to Turn D.C.’s Reflecting Pool Blue
item.description: President Trump handpicked a firm he said had worked on his swimming pool to repair the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial.
item.dc:creator: David A. Fahrenthold and Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Allison Robbert for The New York Times

item.title: Emirates Doubles Down on U.S. and Israeli Ties Amid Iran War
item.description: The United Arab Emirates has borne the brunt of Iranian attacks throughout the war, hardening the government’s resolve to reassess its list of friends and foes.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: The Emirates leader, Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed, and President Trump in Abu Dhabi, last year.

item.title: Iran Says It Seized Oil Tanker Listed by U.S. as Sanctions Violator
item.description: A maritime tracking company said the move was likely performative, given the Chinese-owned ship has an “established history within the Iranian trade ecosystem.”
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Ships in the Strait of Hormuz near Oman on Friday.

item.title: ABC Accuses Government of Violating First Amendment
item.description: The network’s argument, made to the F.C.C., is the most aggressive posture taken yet by a television network toward the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Jim Rutenberg and John Koblin
item.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The filing on Friday was registered on behalf of a single ABC station in Houston and involved a minor regulatory dispute over the talk show “The View,” which regularly hosts politicians and cultural figures.

item.title: Pentagon Releases Files on U.F.O.s
item.description: The initial files are murky images that show what could be anything. The government said more would be released on a rolling basis.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Trump’s Latest Tariff Setback Looms Over China Talks
item.description: For the president, a major legal defeat could undercut his leverage as he prepares to head to Beijing next week for high-stakes trade negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Ana Swanson
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader, in 2025. Tariffs are expected to be a major topic when they meet next week in Beijing.

item.title: David Attenborough, a Voice of Nature, Turns 100
item.description: Pictures and striking scenes from the making of perhaps the world’s most celebrated naturalist.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:credit: John Stillwell/Press Association, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Mr. Attenborough enrolled at the University of Cambridge in 1945, where he studied natural sciences, including geology and zoology.

item.title: What to know about the report.
item.description: Employers added 115,000 jobs and the unemployment rate remained at 4.3 percent despite higher energy prices and instability spurred by the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Ember
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Federal Reserve Turns Focus to Inflation as Job Market Stabilizes
item.description: April’s report showed employers added more jobs than expected, supporting the central bank’s view that it can afford to hold interest rates steady.
item.dc:creator: Colby Smith
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie/The New York Times
item.media:description: Higher prices could eventually temper consumer spending, causing a slowdown that could result in job losses.

item.title: ‘Fired From Retirement’: Financially Insecure, Older Americans Return to the Grind
item.description: People return to work for many reasons, but the biggest is financial need. As costs remain high, more retirees could be looking to re-enter the job market.
item.dc:creator: Kurtis Lee
item.category: Center for Retirement Research at Boston College
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times
item.media:description: Pat Archer retired several years ago but found that her Social Security and small pension couldn’t keep up with her bills. She found part-time work — in a retirement home.

item.title: Compare the Purported Epstein Suicide Note to His Writings
item.description: The New York Times has not authenticated the note. But some of its phrases are similar or identical to ones Jeffrey Epstein used in emails and another handwritten letter.
item.dc:creator: Steve Eder and Eve Edelheit
item.media:credit: New York State Sex Offender Registry, via Associated Press

item.title: Experts Stress Hantavirus Risk Remains Low As Officials Track Cruise Ship Passengers
item.description: Health officials are closely monitoring those who were exposed for symptoms of the virus, which does not spread easily between humans.
item.dc:creator: Dani Blum
item.media:credit: Getty Images

item.title: Hantavirus Is Nothing Like Coronavirus, but It’s Bringing Some ‘Covid P.T.S.D.’
item.description: Experts have been quick to reassure the public after the deaths aboard the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius, but images and turns of phrase have rekindled anxieties from Covid’s early days.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Misper Apawu/Associated Press
item.media:description: Health workers in protective gear evacuating patients from the MV Hondius cruise ship yesterday.

item.title: Before Hantavirus Outbreak, the MV Hondius Attracted ‘a Different Type of Traveler’
item.description: Former passengers on the MV Hondius say that the ship was made for wildlife expeditions and that the crew took safety very seriously.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel, Claire Moses and Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The MV Hondius off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday. Passengers on the ship showed symptoms of the hantavirus, and some died.

item.title: What to Know About the Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
item.description: Health authorities said three passengers from the MV Hondius had died after showing symptoms of the rare disease.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses and Jin Yu Young
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: The cruise ship MV Hondius stationed off the port of Praia, Cape Verde, on Sunday.

item.title: Meta’s Embrace of A.I. Is Making Its Employees Miserable
item.description: As it adapts to the artificial intelligence era, the company is pushing many of its 78,000 workers to use the technology, and preparing to lay some of them off.
item.dc:creator: Kalley Huang, Eli Tan and Kate Conger
item.media:credit: Doug Chayka

item.title: How Prepared Are We for A.I. Layoffs?
item.description: Our chief economics correspondent, Ben Casselman, describes how A.I.-related layoffs are testing the resilience of the government’s safety net programs.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov, Stephanie Swart and Rafaela Balster

item.title: A.I. Populism Is Here. And No One Is Ready.
item.description: Silicon Valley oligarchs worried about the risks their technology posed to the world. They forgot about people.
item.dc:creator: David Wallace-Wells

item.title: A Gun Derailed My Childhood. As an Adult, I Found Relief at the Range.
item.description: The guilt of my teenage conviction haunted me for decades. Learning to shoot helped me forgive myself as an adult.
item.dc:creator: Reginald Dwayne Betts
item.media:credit: Brian Finke for The New York Times
item.media:description: Reginald Dwayne Betts at the Sharpshooters Group gun range in April.

item.title: What’s at Stake in the UK’s Local Elections
item.description: The voting in England, Scotland and Wales is the biggest electoral test for Keir Starmer since he became prime minister in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:credit: Leon Neal/Getty Images
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain spoke on Friday after local election results began rolling in.

item.title: 7 Busy Parents Share Their Quickest Go-To Dinners
item.description: End-of-school year chaos has arrived. These 20-minute (or less) meals will get you cooking through it.
item.dc:creator: Margaux Laskey
item.media:credit: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini. Prop Stylist: Megan Hedgpeth.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg Is Running Meta Into the Ground
item.description: Meta has commenced a long, slow slide into irrelevance.
item.dc:creator: Julia Angwin

item.title: The Iran War Will Cost Trillions
item.description: The war in Iran will cost trillions — far more than current estimates.
item.dc:creator: Justin Wolfers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Going for Broke
item.description: Why does the public seem unbothered by the national debt?
item.dc:creator: Evan Gorelick
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times

item.title: Can They Build ‘America First’ Without Nick Fuentes?
item.description: A small group of candidates and influencers met to forge a shaky new political movement — without its de facto leader and his groypers.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Taylor Pemberton
item.media:credit: Brian Kaiser for The New York Times
item.media:description: On a Saturday afternoon in May, an eclectic group that included Casey Putsch, a Republican running for governor in Ohio, tried to find common ground under the America First banner.

item.title: Festus Mogae, Former Botswana President Who Tackled H.I.V., Dies at 86
item.description: He led the southern African nation for a decade and embraced a flagship American aid program credited with helping to eradicate the country’s AIDS epidemic.
item.dc:creator: Yvonne Mooka and John Eligon
item.category: President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief
item.media:credit: Joao Silva for The New York Times
item.media:description: Festus Mogae in 2004, during his decade as president of Botswana. He fought the country’s H.I.V./AIDS crisis and presided over robust economic growth because of diamond production.

item.title: Four Men Found Guilty of Haiti President’s Assassination
item.description: President Jovenel Moïse, 53, was gunned down in his bedroom in July 2021. His killing unleashed a yearslong spiral of gang violence and mayhem in Haiti.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams
item.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural of Jovenel Moïse near his home in Port-au-Prince, Haiti, where he was assassinated in 2021.

item.title: The French Couple That Tried to Burgle 29 Churches in 3 Months
item.description: A spree of thefts last summer heightened alarm in France over protection of cultural and rural heritage.
item.dc:creator: Catherine Porter and Ana Castelain
item.media:credit: Andrea Mantovani for The New York Times
item.media:description: The tabernacle in the church of Chamouille, France, which was robbed last summer. Some priests oversee as many as 50 churches.
